commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh Say not, I will do so to him as he has done to me: I will render to the man according to his work. Whose father. Jerome has read ab avi, instead of abo, (Calmet) which is an interjection, (Bo chart) alas. or it means, "trouble. "Septuagint, "drunkenness "(Chaldean; Calmet) or "sorrow. "(Protestants) Falls. Septuagint, "hath sorrows. "Hebrew, "babbling "(Protestants; Haydock) or discontents of mind. (Calmet) Cause. Drunkards often fall upon their best friends, as Alexander did on Clytus. (Menochius).
